content.text: A design gap is something the docs forgot. A parked item is something the docs refused. This register is the published index; the working flags ledger stays the working document.

Five items were parked by name in the rebuild canon; one — the Keth multi-arm interaction — has since been resolved by designer ruling, and its entry below now points at the page that owns the design. The handling rule for the rest, printed in the Phase 7 handoff and binding on every page: carry old mechanics as pending redesign where they exist, flag the spot on the live page, and never improvise. Parked items stay parked until a designer ruling unparks them — flagging the need at the table is correct play; designing it there is not.

content.title: Multi-Armed Combat — the Keth
content.subtitle: Titan Grip / Dual Swing interaction — RESOLVED 2026-06

entries.label: Status
entries.value: RESOLVED 2026-06 by designer ruling (races-v2). It landed on The Keth: the four-arm gear and Dual Swing rules, the native Titan Grip gates, and the costs that pay for them all live there. Dual Wield & Titan Grip now carries a pointer instead of a PARKED flag.

entries.label: What it was
entries.value: How a four-armed body interacts with Dual Swing, Titan Grip's Chrome gates, and shield use. While parked, the pre-rebuild four-arm mechanics were preserved on The Keth for reference only — they came down with the ruling.

entries.label: Why parked
entries.value: The Titan Grip ruling filed the interaction rather than ruling it — "parked, on file" — and the Phase 7 handoff marks it deliberately undesigned: the Keth's mechanics predate the rebuild, and the race awaits redesign under the new grammar.

entries.label: Flags retired
entries.value: Dual Wield & Titan Grip (PARKED) · The Keth (PENDING REDESIGN) · Playable Species (PENDING REDESIGN) — all removed with the ruling.

entries.label: Unblocked by
entries.value: The Keth redesign ruling — delivered as races-v2 (2026-06). The items filed with it dissolved: four-arm combat is designed under the new grammar, and the new design touches neither the deleted Speed stat nor Edge.

entries.label: What it is
entries.value: Facility tiers, expansions, staff, defenses — anything that makes the base a character instead of a discount. The low-tier module itself is live (The Base, GM Core), governed by the funnel law: the base owns the floor of every funnel; people own the top.

entries.label: Why parked
entries.value: Scoped post-core and low-tier from the start — hooks reserved, content deferred — so the expensive bands stay relationships.

entries.label: Flags on live pages
entries.value: The Base (PARKED)

entries.label: Unblocked by
entries.value: Playtests — the module plan holds base content low-tier until then. Phase 6 prices base hooks floor-band only, per the funnel law.

entries.label: What it is
entries.value: The vehicle customization catalog: hidden compartments (gate inspections), freezer units (the preservation clock), extended fuel (convoy legs), and Seal upgrades.

entries.label: Why parked
entries.value: The master consolidation parks the entire customization pass. Vehicles shipped as combat-and-logistics chassis; the catalog is its own future pass.

entries.label: Flags on live pages
entries.value: Vehicles, GM Core (PARKED) · Mods (PARKED) · Rigger (DESIGN GAP — Chop Access and Smuggler's Frame already price against the unwritten catalog)

entries.label: Unblocked by
entries.value: The customization catalog pass. Standing dependency to resolve with it: two printed Rigger perks lean on the catalog existing.

entries.label: What it is
entries.value: Any pattern beyond the launch set. The ten on Patterns are the whole arsenal.

entries.label: Why parked
entries.value: The Phase 7 handoff parks the catalog beyond the launch ten by name — flagged for a future pass, not designed.

entries.label: Flags on live pages
entries.value: Patterns (PARKED)

entries.label: Unblocked by
entries.value: A pattern catalog pass. Note that the launch ten themselves carry open calibration gaps — per-pattern Weave DCs, round counts, crit beats, flagged on Patterns — which precede any expansion.

entries.label: What it is
entries.value: Exotic companions and Bloom-stock creatures beyond the Bestiary's statted roster. The Influence beast table reserves their row: priced like the contraband they are.

entries.label: Why parked
entries.value: Deferred from the Influence catalog to the opposition phase, which shipped its roster without them. Deliberately undesigned since — the Bestiary's printed entries are everything that exists.

entries.label: Flags on live pages
entries.value: Influence (PARKED) · Bestiary (PARKED)

entries.label: Unblocked by
entries.value: A bestiary expansion pass carrying the contraband pricing line. Standing dependency: the Wrangler's Exotic License perk already licenses Bloom-stock handling, Green Cradle audit Obligation attached.

content.text: Distinct from parked: a design gap is a hole the regeneration found in the canon. Every gap is flagged where it bites — a DESIGN GAP callout on the owning page — and recorded, numbered, in the Phase 7 flags ledger, which remains the working document for review. The themes, at summary level:

The economy's missing anchors. No mission pay scale exists in any rebuild doc — the largest single hole, since every drain is priced against income that is never printed. Alongside it: rent and lifestyle, weapon prices, splice purchase prices, repair and reforge pricing, scar treatment costs, intel prices, base facility costs, per-item crafting times and base list prices. (Flagged on The Economy Dials, Weapons, Mutations & Grafts, Armor, Intel, The Base, The Material Cost Law.)

The attack-governing stat. No document assigns which stat governs attacks with a given weapon — confirmed open after the Weapons audit and flagged prominently there. Every combat page writes around it.
Unprinted character baselines. Starting HP, the starting stat array, starting RP before a fight's first activation, the quick-action list, the stabilize DC.

Unsized riders and conditions. Seared X, the concealment accuracy modifier, the Predictable chip tag, leverage tags (gap stands; future home assigned on Leverage & Encounter Play), the carry-it scar debuffs.
Procedure holes. The ambush round, forced vent at Charge 10, the wild mutation table, field repair's permanent consequence, the OFF-dial's scar-class Strain.

GM-side calibration. Per-role enemy ability menus and activation budgets below bosses, boss quantification (Poise splits, Elite-plus deltas), node traversal costs and the mission clock, the intel DC setting rule, a worked briefing template, carving DCs by creature tier and preservation windows, vehicle chassis numbers (EV caps, Capacity, prices, Char fatigue, Seal thresholds).
Catalog shortfalls. Shadow under the density standard; Leech's Burn stack duration; the pattern calibration set above; per-weapon crit payouts awaiting the beat budget.

The races translation. Closed 2026-06 by designer ruling (races-v2): species mechanics are now rebuild canon, printed on the species pages — the Keth unparked (above), the Human XP-to-LP question answered in LP terms, the Vael and Grunt rebuilt without the flagged dependencies. One pricing dependency remains: the Flicker's max-HP delta prices against the still-unprinted starting HP (see Unprinted character baselines, above).

Consistency calls held for review. The Marked name collision (combat condition vs Saturation stage), misfire wording on corrupt rounds and lost patterns, downtime Charge-venting timing, the Dual Swing Evasion floor for light pairs, and the splice roll's Grit multiplier sitting at half the death roll's.

The ledger, not this page, is where gaps get worked. When a gap closes, the owning page loses its callout and this list shrinks; when a parked item unparks, its entry above becomes a pointer to the page that owns the new design.
